[{"title": "[DEEP] 8 startups reshaping the global food system - AgFunderNews", "url": "https://agfundernews.com/8-global-startups-reshaping-the-global-food-system", "snippet": "Title: 8 startups reshaping the global food system URL Source: https://agfundernews.com/8-global-startups-reshaping-the-global-food-system Published Time: 2021-05-05T09:00:19+00:00 Markdown Content: _Editor\u2019s note: This post is part of a collaboration with[F&A Next](http://www.fanext.com/), an[AgFunder Network Partner](https://agfundernews.com/agfunder-network-partners). F&A Next was founded by[Anterra Capital](http://www.anterracapital.com/),[StartLife](https://start-life.nl/),[Rabobank](https://www.rabobankamerica.com/), and[Wageningen University & Research](https://www.wur.nl/en.htm)._ _F&A Next is a platform dedicated to connecting food and agriculture startups to investors, serving as a springboard to greater innovation and a better deal for all. It organizes an annual conference in May, which will be virtual again in 2021. Throughout the day, the forum will showcase eight of the \u201cNext Heroes\u201d startups in agrifoodtech._ _Find out more [here](https://www.fanext.com/), and read about last year\u2019s event[here](https://agfundernews.com/sexy-supply-chain-tech-a-next-2020.html). Apply the code**AFN-FaNext21**to benefit from a 25% discount!_ When things got tough in 2020, innovators got going. And investors noticed. Agrifoodtech innovation and investing had a breakout year last year, raking in more than $30 billion. Early-stage ventures did particularly well: dollars committed was up 10% and deal count was up 15% from 2019. Showcasing the range of agrifoodtech innovators who toiled away in spite of the pandemic (and even started their businesses amid a global lockdown), eight startups will present their products and ideas at F&A Next\u2019s virtual summit on May 26.Their solutions, which address food waste, remote sensing, new sources of nutrients and proteins, and more, exemplify the creativity and ingenuity that has global investors eager to place their bets on the agrifoodtech sector at large. Here\u2019s a sneak peak at who they are. #### **BiomilQ** US-based [BiomilQ](https://www.biomilq.com/) has been in the cellular foods game for a long time. But it isn\u2019t making burgers or fish fillets. It\u2019s making lab-grown human breast milk. Many women aren\u2019t able to breast feed, and their most available alternative is bottle-based formula made of cow, goat or plant-based milks. Those don\u2019t have the same nutrients as the milk that mothers produce. The women-led company closed a $3.5 million seed round last summer and grew its team from two to 15, at the height of global lockdowns. BiomilQ has since been working to optimize its cell culture media and characterize mammary cells. Founder Michelle Egger says the company has also made advances in reducing its production costs. The company is committed to making a product that is affordable for mothers of all income levels. The company is eying 2022 as a milestone year, says Egger. \u201cWe are constructing our own manufacturing plant and look forward to creating product at scale that can enter regulatory safety testing.\u201d In the meantime, BiomilQ is engaged in consumer education to teach parents about lab-grown breast milk. \u201cMost parents and incredibly open to the product and category of human milk, but we know there is a lot of unscientific information out there that muddles the discussion,\u201d Egger says. #### **Ful Foods**\n...\nTitle: 8 startups reshaping the global food system URL Source: https://agfundernews.com/8-global-startups-reshaping-the-global-food-system Published Time: 2021-05-05T09:00:19+00:00 Markdown Cont...", "is_deep": true}, {"title": "Research on time-varying path optimization for multi-vehicle type fresh food logistics distribution considering energy consumption | Scientific Reports", "url": "https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-78639-1", "snippet": "November 7, 2024 -Scientific Reports - Research on time-varying path optimization for multi-vehicle type fresh food logistics distribution considering energy consumption", "is_deep": false}, {"title": "Optimization of Fresh Food Logistics Routes for Heterogeneous Fleets in Segmented Transshipment Mode | MDPI", "url": "https://www.mdpi.com/2227-7390/12/23/3831", "snippet": "December 4, 2024 -To address the challenges of environmental impact and distribution efficiency in fresh food logistics,a segmented transshipment model involving the coordinated operation of gasoline and electric vehicles is proposed.", "is_deep": false}, {"title": "FReSH: transforming global food systems", "url": "https://wbcsd.org/Programs/Food-and-Nature/Food-Land-Use/FReSH/FReSH-transforming-global-food-systems", "snippet": "September 30, 2025 -Driving advocacy for an ambitious transition by sharing policy insights, enabling engagement with policymakers and influencing global agenda in agriculture and food.", "is_deep": false}]
